definition_origin: End-to-end asset workflow = the full pipeline from emotional brief to ship-ready asset in repo: Concept → Generate → Refine → Optimize → Integrate → Animate → Maintain. The workflow exists because solo dev with zero design skill needs a deterministic process to produce branded assets at scale without becoming a designer. Each stage references prior outputs and feeds the next, with archetype-spec.md and tokens.json as the persistent backbone.
core_purpose: VISCERAL — every asset that ships actually carries the brand archetype because the workflow injects it at every step. BEHAVIORAL — the pipeline is a discipline, not inspiration; output is predictable. REFLECTIVE — the system reduces 'shipping shame' (the feeling that you cut corners on visuals) and replaces it with craft pride. For solo dev, the workflow is the difference between '6 hours per asset and inconsistent' and '90 minutes per asset and on-brand'.
when_needed: Mandatory once asset count crosses ~5 unique pieces. Before that, ad-hoc is fine. Critical when launching a brand, scaling a feature, or onboarding a contractor. Less critical for one-shot marketing campaigns with disposable assets, but even there the workflow's first 3 steps (concept → moodboard → AI gen) save time.
format_options: Each stage produces stage-appropriate format: Concept = .md docs (archetype-spec, emotional-spec, anti-tokens). Generate = .png raw AI output, .json for Suno stems. Refine = .psd / .procreate / .kra working files (NOT shipped). Optimize = .svg / .webp / .avif / .mp3 / .lottie / .dotlottie production formats. Integrate = TypeScript imports, repo paths. Animate = Lottie .json or .lottie, Rive .riv, native Reanimated code. Maintain = updates to tokens.json + character-bible.md + prompt-library.md. The workflow itself is a doc: pipeline.md.
file_size_performance: Each stage has a target: raw AI output (any size), refined master (10-50MB working files OK), optimized production (target <100KB hero / <30KB icon / <500KB Lottie). Performance failure = skipping the optimize stage and shipping the raw AI output (3MB png hero kills LCP). The workflow ENFORCES the optimize stage as a CI gate — pre-commit hook fails on .png > 200KB without /raw/ prefix.
free_tools: STAGE 1 CONCEPT — Gemini Pro (Imagen 4 + Whisk + NotebookLM); Pinterest / Are.na (moodboards); Coolors (palettes); Google Fonts. STAGE 2 GENERATE — Imagen 4 via Gemini (free for Pro subs); Recraft (30 free credits/day, SVG); Krea AI (free tier); Whisk until April 2026; Suno free (50 credits/day); ElevenLabs free (60 SFX/mo, 10k chars TTS); Stable Audio Open (local, non-commercial). STAGE 3 REFINE — Photopea (free Photoshop in browser); Krita (free desktop painting); Inkscape (free vector); Figma free; Penpot free; Audacity (free DAW); GIMP. STAGE 4 OPTIMIZE — SVGO (free CLI/web); Squoosh (free Chrome Labs web tool); LottieFiles optimizer / TinyLottie; ffmpeg (free CLI for audio/video); ImageOptim (Mac). STAGE 5 INTEGRATE — VS Code + Claude Code + git; Style Dictionary; ESLint custom rules. STAGE 6 ANIMATE — LottieFiles community + creator tools; Rive free tier (free community / free indie under $10k revenue); Reanimated (free OSS); Framer Motion (free OSS). STAGE 7 MAINTAIN — GitHub free; Storybook / Ladle free; Chromatic free tier.
paid_tools: STARTER ($10-15/mo): Figma Professional $15/seat (better Variables + library). Procreate $13 one-time iPad (best refine tool for hand-drawn). Suno Pro $10/mo (commercial rights + 500 songs/mo). Spline free is enough for 3D; Spline Pro $9/mo for export. PRO ($20-30/mo): Midjourney $30/mo (better archetype lock than free generators). ElevenLabs Creator $22/mo (commercial voice + unlimited SFX). Rive Pro $24/mo (advanced state machines). ENTERPRISE ($50+/mo): hire freelance illustrator $200-500/piece on Working Not Working / Dribbble. The free stack covers 100% of the pipeline; paid only when revenue justifies.
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro is the WORKFLOW BACKBONE: (1) STAGE 1 CONCEPT — feed business idea + target user + competitor screenshots; ask 'derive 12-archetype scoring; pick primary + secondary; produce emotional_spec.md (emotional promise, do/don't, archetype-aligned tokens)'. Output goes to /design/. (2) STAGE 2 GENERATE — paste emotional_spec.md + asset request; ask 'generate 5 Imagen 4 prompts' THEN call Imagen 4 directly via Gemini. Use Whisk for style-anchored variations. Suno prompts written here. ElevenLabs SFX prompts written here. (3) STAGE 3 REFINE — paste raw AI output + emotional_spec; ask 'audit for AI-slop tells, list specific cleanup steps'. Use NotebookLM as a 'is this on-brand?' oracle. (4) STAGE 5 INTEGRATE — paste asset path + RN code; ask 'generate type-safe import + reduce-motion fallback + accessibility alt text'. (5) STAGE 7 MAINTAIN — quarterly audit: 'review tokens.json + assets folder; flag drift, redundancy, retired tokens, missing reduce-motion variants'. (6) Use Gemini's 1M context window to keep emotional_spec + tokens.json + character-bible loaded across all asset sessions for consistency.
workflow_steps: STAGE 1 CONCEPT (2-3 hr ONCE per brand, then 15 min per asset): Lock archetype + emotional spec → moodboard 30 references → write asset brief (1 paragraph: what, why, where, archetype-anchor). STAGE 2 GENERATE (30-60 min per asset): write archetype-locked prompt referencing emotional_spec → run in Imagen 4 / Recraft / Krea / Suno / ElevenLabs → keep top 5 candidates from 30+ generations. STAGE 3 REFINE (30-90 min per asset): hand-edit in Procreate / Photopea / Inkscape / Audacity → fix AI-slop tells (melted hands, gradient mesh, generic chibi) → align to archetype tokens → save working master in /design/raw/ (gitignored). STAGE 4 OPTIMIZE (10-20 min per asset): run SVGO for vector → Squoosh for raster → TinyLottie for animation → ffmpeg for audio. Verify file size < target. STAGE 5 INTEGRATE (10-15 min per asset): name per /design/asset-naming.md → drop into /apps/mobile/assets/ → add type-safe import → reference via tokens. STAGE 6 ANIMATE (30-90 min if needed): build Lottie / Rive / Reanimated state. STAGE 7 MAINTAIN (1 hr/quarter): audit drift, update tokens, retire unused, document new prompt template variants. TOTAL per asset for solo dev with zero design skill: ~90 min for static illustration / ~3 hr for animated mascot pose / ~30 min for SFX. After 10-20 assets the per-asset time drops 30-50% as the prompt library + workflow muscle-memory builds.
ai_prompt_templates: STAGE 1 brief generation: 'Given product = {PRODUCT}, archetype primary = {A1}, archetype secondary = {A2}, target user = {USER}, surface = {SURFACE}, generate an asset brief: 1) emotional intent (1 sentence), 2) format (SVG/Lottie/PNG/MP3), 3) target file size, 4) archetype tokens to use (color/font/style), 5) anti-tokens (slop to avoid), 6) reference images (3 URLs), 7) success criteria (3 bullets).' STAGE 2 generation prompt template: 'A {ARCHETYPE_STYLE} {ASSET_TYPE} of {SUBJECT}. Style: {ARTIST_REFERENCE}. Palette: {HEX_LIST}. NO {ANTI_TOKENS_LIST}. {COMPOSITION_NOTES}. Transparent background. {FORMAT_REQUIREMENTS}.' STAGE 3 audit prompt: 'Audit this raw AI output (image attached) against emotional_spec.md (pasted). List: 1) on-brand strengths, 2) AI-slop tells present, 3) tokens violations, 4) specific Procreate/Krita actions to fix in <30 min.' STAGE 5 integrate prompt to Claude Code: 'Add asset {PATH} to /apps/mobile/. Generate type-safe import, JSX usage example, reduce-motion fallback, alt text for screen readers. Update tokens.json if new color introduced.'
claude_code_integration: Repo layout: /design/{emotional_spec.md, archetype-spec.md, character-bible.md, anti-tokens.md, prompt-library/, raw/ (gitignored)}; /packages/shared/src/tokens/; /apps/mobile/assets/{icons,illustrations,animations,audio,3d}/. Scripts: pnpm asset:optimize (SVGO + Squoosh + ffmpeg + TinyLottie batch); pnpm asset:audit (file-size enforcement + naming validation + token compliance check); pnpm tokens:build (Style Dictionary). Pre-commit: lint-staged runs SVGO and audit on staged files. Claude Code slash-commands: /asset-brief (generates STAGE 1 brief from current archetype-spec); /asset-prompt (generates STAGE 2 prompt for given asset type); /asset-audit (runs STAGE 3 audit on attached image); /asset-integrate (STAGE 5 with type-safe import generation). PR template includes 'asset workflow checklist': brief written? prompt referenced tokens? optimized? integrated with alt text?
emotional_design_integration: The workflow ITSELF embeds emotional design at every stage. STAGE 1 concept = REFLECTIVE level (brand pride, archetype declaration). STAGE 2 generate = VISCERAL level (first-impression intent). STAGE 3 refine = VISCERAL polish + BEHAVIORAL utility. STAGE 4 optimize = BEHAVIORAL (performance is emotional — 100ms LCP penalty drops trust). STAGE 5 integrate = BEHAVIORAL (consistent placement). STAGE 6 animate = VISCERAL (motion personality) + BEHAVIORAL (feedback). STAGE 7 maintain = REFLECTIVE (long-term brand pride). Skipping any stage drops emotional fidelity. Per archetype the workflow weighting differs: SAGE/RULER weight stages 3-4 heaviest (refine + optimize for restraint); JESTER/MAGICIAN weight stages 2 + 6 heaviest (generation variety + animation expression); CAREGIVER weights stage 3 heaviest (hand-edit warmth). Solo dev should not skip any stage but can compress duration per archetype.
specific_examples: Real workflow case studies: (1) Linear's icon system (https://linear.app/) — concept locked early, Pablo Stanley illustration, refined repeatedly, version controlled in repo. (2) Stripe's hero illustrations (https://stripe.com/) — workflow includes external commission stage, but tokens + integration similar. (3) Headspace's design system (https://standards.site/case-studies/headspace/) — public workflow docs. (4) Excalidraw's hand-drawn library (https://excalidraw.com/) — built workflow that locks the rough-draft aesthetic so all icons match. (5) Notion mascot illustrations — single illustrator's locked style across thousands of pieces. Solo-dev case studies: (6) Pieter Levels apps (Nomad List, Photo AI) — minimal asset workflow but ruthlessly consistent. (7) Tony Dinh apps (Black Magic, DevUtils) — single hand-drawn aesthetic across all apps because of locked workflow. (8) Marc Lou's apps (ShipFast templates) — template approach that bakes the workflow into the codebase.
anti_ai_slop_traps: (1) Skipping STAGE 1 concept = AI defaults to slop because no archetype constraint applied. Counter: brief is non-negotiable. (2) Skipping STAGE 3 refine = raw AI output ships = obvious slop. Counter: every raw output gets 30-90 min hand-edit. (3) Skipping STAGE 4 optimize = files too large = poor performance = perceived as unpolished. Counter: CI gate. (4) Treating workflow as linear-once instead of iterative — STAGE 7 maintain feeds back into STAGE 1 brief refinement. (5) Letting AI generate STAGE 1 entirely without human archetype lock — Gemini will pick generic archetype. Counter: human picks archetype, AI elaborates only. (6) Not maintaining a prompt-library — each session reinvents prompts and drifts. Counter: every session pulls from /design/prompt-library/ first.
common_pitfalls: (1) Trying to skip stages 'because solo dev' — ironically solo dev needs the workflow MORE not less. (2) Buying paid tools before exhausting free stack. (3) Not gitignoring raw working files — repo bloats with PSDs and Procreate files. (4) Refining endlessly without shipping — set 90 min hard cap per asset. (5) Not budgeting maintain time — quarterly audits are non-optional after 6 months. (6) Generating too many candidates (200) and decision-paralysis. Set 30 candidates max. (7) Letting Lottie/Rive scope creep — animation often eats 5x its budget; cap at 3 animated assets per release until library matures. (8) Forgetting accessibility through optimization — reduce-motion variant should be MADE during STAGE 6 not retrofitted. (9) Not versioning the prompt library — old prompts drift, new prompts orphaned. Treat /design/ as code.
accessibility_notes: Bake accessibility into every stage: STAGE 1 brief includes alt text intent; STAGE 5 integrate includes alt text + reduce-motion variant; STAGE 6 animate ALWAYS produces reduce-motion fallback simultaneously. Color contrast checked at STAGE 4 optimize. Touch-target validation at STAGE 5. Voice-over assets get auto-transcripts at STAGE 4. Tokens encode minimum-contrast pairs (token.text.on-primary tested 4.5:1 AA). Storybook/Ladle gallery surfaces accessibility metadata for human review.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile workflow integration: STAGE 5 produces RN-friendly imports (require() for static, Asset.fromModule for dynamic, lottie-react-native or @lottiefiles/dotlottie-react-native for animation, expo-av or expo-audio for sound, react-native-haptic-feedback for haptics paired with audio). STAGE 4 optimize includes 1x/2x/3x for retina. Web workflow integration: STAGE 5 produces ESM imports, CSS background-image, ,
sources: [Style Dictionary on GitHub](https://github.com/amzn/style-dictionary) — multi-platform token export. [SVGO on GitHub](https://github.com/svg/svgo) — SVG optimization. [Squoosh by Chrome Labs](https://squoosh.app/) — image optimization browser tool. [TinyLottie](https://tiny-lottie.vercel.app/) — Lottie + dotLottie optimization. [LottieFiles dotLottie spec](https://dotlottie.io/) — efficient distribution format. [lottie-react-native (Expo)](https://docs.expo.dev/versions/latest/sdk/lottie/) — RN integration. [Rive](https://rive.app/) — interactive animation tool. [Recraft AI Vector](https://www.recraft.ai/ai-vector-generator) — SVG generation. [Suno AI Pricing 2026](https://suno.com/pricing) — music generation tiers. [ElevenLabs Sound Effects](https://elevenlabs.io/sound-effects) — SFX + voice generation. [Penpot 1.19 design tokens](https://penpot.app/blog/7-reasons-penpot-is-more-than-just-a-figma-alternative/) — open-source workflow alternative. [Inhaq: Figma to Code Design Tokens 2026](https://inhaq.com/blog/figma-to-code-design-engineer-workflow) — modern integration patterns. [Blakecrosley Headspace guide](https://blakecrosley.com/guides/design/headspace) — workflow case study.
